body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 22/2026
Made under: Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: March 9, 2026

B.C. Reg. 22/2026, deposited March 9, 2026, under theATTORNEY GENERAL STATUTES AMENDMENT ACT (No. 2), 2025[section 25]. Order in Council 66/2026, approved and ordered March 9, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective June 15, 2026, sections 3 to 24 of theAttorney General Statutes Amendment Act (No. 2), 2025, S.B.C. 2025, c. 25, are brought into force. — N. SHARMA,Attorney General and Deputy Premier; M. FARNWORTH,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act sections 3–24 take effect June 15, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Sections 3 to 24 of the Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act (No. 2), 2025 will come into force on June 15, 2026. This means several changes to laws overseen by BC's Attorney General will become active, potentially affecting how businesses handle contracts, disputes, or other legal matters. The exact changes depend on which statutes are amended by those sections.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review sections 3–24 of the Act to identify which specific laws affect your business and prepare for compliance by June 15, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act sections 3–24 take effect
